docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A 2D rectangle-shaped bounded area (bounding box).

There are a number of convenience functions to get positions and points on the Bounds. Currently we do not store these with the Bounds2 instance, since we want to lower the memory footprint.

minX, minY, maxX, and maxY are actually stored. We don't do x,y,width,height because this can't properly express semi-infinite bounds (like a half-plane), or easily handle what Bounds2.NOTHING and Bounds2.EVERYTHING do with the constructive solid areas.

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: Class Bounds2
docs.text: import { Bounds2 } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: new Bounds2( minX : number, minY : number, maxX : number, maxY : number )
getWidth() : number
docs.text: The width of the bounds, defined as maxX - minX.

docs.title: getHeight() : number
docs.text: The height of the bounds, defined as maxY - minY.

docs.title: getX() : number
docs.text: Alias for minX, when thinking of the bounds as an (x,y,width,height) rectangle.

docs.title: getY() : number
docs.text: Alias for minY, when thinking of the bounds as an (x,y,width,height) rectangle.

docs.title: getMinX() : number
docs.text: Alias for minX, supporting the explicit getter function style.

docs.title: getMinY() : number
docs.text: Alias for minY, supporting the explicit getter function style.

docs.title: getMaxX() : number
docs.text: Alias for maxX, supporting the explicit getter function style.

docs.title: getMaxY() : number
docs.text: Alias for maxY, supporting the explicit getter function style.

docs.title: getLeft() : number
docs.text: Alias for minX, when thinking in the UI-layout manner.

docs.title: getTop() : number
docs.text: Alias for minY, when thinking in the UI-layout manner.

docs.title: getRight() : number
docs.text: Alias for maxX, when thinking in the UI-layout manner.

docs.title: getBottom() : number
docs.text: Alias for maxY, when thinking in the UI-layout manner.

docs.title: getCenterX() : number
docs.text: The horizontal (X-coordinate) center of the bounds, averaging the minX and maxX.

docs.title: getCenterY() : number
docs.text: The vertical (Y-coordinate) center of the bounds, averaging the minY and maxY.

docs.title: getLeftTop() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (minX, minY), in the UI-coordinate upper-left.

docs.title: getCenterTop() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (centerX, minY), in the UI-coordinate upper-center.

docs.title: getRightTop() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (right, minY), in the UI-coordinate upper-right.

docs.title: getLeftCenter() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (left, centerY), in the UI-coordinate center-left.

docs.title: getCenter() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (centerX, centerY), in the center of the bounds.

docs.title: getRightCenter() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (maxX, centerY), in the UI-coordinate center-right

docs.title: getLeftBottom() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (minX, maxY), in the UI-coordinate lower-left

docs.title: getCenterBottom() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (centerX, maxY), in the UI-coordinate lower-center

docs.title: getRightBottom() : Vector2
docs.text: The point (maxX, maxY), in the UI-coordinate lower-right

docs.title: isEmpty() : boolean
docs.text: Whether we have negative width or height. Bounds2.NOTHING is a prime example of an empty Bounds2. Bounds with width = height = 0 are considered not empty, since they include the single (0,0) point.

docs.title: isFinite() : boolean
docs.text: Whether our minimums and maximums are all finite numbers. This will exclude Bounds2.NOTHING and Bounds2.EVERYTHING.

docs.title: hasNonzeroArea() : boolean
docs.text: Whether this bounds has a non-zero area (non-zero positive width and height).

docs.title: isValid() : boolean
docs.text: Whether this bounds has a finite and non-negative width and height.

docs.title: closestPointTo( point : Vector2 ) : Vector2
docs.text: If the point is inside the bounds, the point will be returned. Otherwise, this will return a new point on the edge of the bounds that is the closest to the provided point.

docs.title: closestBoundaryPointTo( point : Vector2 ) : Vector2
docs.text: Find the point on the boundary of the Bounds2 that is closest to the provided point.

docs.title: getConstrainedPoint( point : Vector2 ) : Vector2
docs.text: Give a point outside of this Bounds2, constrain it to a point on the boundary of this Bounds2.

docs.title: containsCoordinates( x : number, y : number ) : boolean
docs.text:
Whether the coordinates are contained inside the bounding box, or are on the boundary.

@param x - X coordinate of the point to check @param y - Y coordinate of the point to check

docs.title: containsPoint( point : Vector2 ) : boolean
docs.text: Whether the point is contained inside the bounding box, or is on the boundary.

docs.title: containsBounds( bounds : Bounds2 ) : boolean
docs.text: Whether this bounding box completely contains the bounding box passed as a parameter. The boundary of a box is considered to be "contained".

docs.title: intersectsBounds( bounds : Bounds2 ) : boolean
docs.text: Whether this and another bounding box have any points of intersection (including touching boundaries).

docs.title: minimumDistanceToPointSquared( point : Vector2 ) : number
docs.text: The squared distance from the input point to the point closest to it inside the bounding box.

docs.title: maximumDistanceToPointSquared( point : Vector2 ) : number
docs.text: The squared distance from the input point to the point furthest from it inside the bounding box.

docs.title: toString() : string
docs.text: Debugging string for the bounds.

docs.title: equals( other : Bounds2 ) : boolean
docs.text:
Exact equality comparison between this bounds and another bounds.

@returns - Whether the two bounds are equal

docs.title: equalsEpsilon( other : Bounds2, epsilon : number ) : boolean
docs.text:
Approximate equality comparison between this bounds and another bounds.

@returns - Whether difference between the two bounds has no min/max with an absolute value greater than epsilon.

docs.title: copy( bounds? : Bounds2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Creates a copy of this bounds, or if a bounds is passed in, set that bounds's values to ours.

This is the immutable form of the function set(), if a bounds is provided. This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

@param [bounds] - If not provided, creates a new Bounds2 with filled in values. Otherwise, fills in the values of the provided bounds so that it equals this bounds.

docs.title: union( bounds : Bounds2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
The smallest bounds that contains both this bounds and the input bounds, returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function includeBounds(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: intersection( bounds : Bounds2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
The smallest bounds that is contained by both this bounds and the input bounds, returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function constrainBounds(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withCoordinates( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
The smallest bounds that contains this bounds and the point (x,y), returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function addCoordinates(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withPoint( point : Vector2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
The smallest bounds that contains this bounds and the input point, returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function addPoint(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Returns the smallest bounds that contains both this bounds and the x value provided.

This is the immutable form of the function addX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Returns the smallest bounds that contains both this bounds and the y value provided.

This is the immutable form of the function addY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withMinX( minX : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with minX replaced with the input.

This is the immutable form of the function setMinX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withMinY( minY : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with minY replaced with the input.

This is the immutable form of the function setMinY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withMaxX( maxX : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with maxX replaced with the input.

This is the immutable form of the function setMaxX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: withMaxY( maxY : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with maxY replaced with the input.

This is the immutable form of the function setMaxY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: roundedOut() : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with the minimum values rounded down to the nearest integer, and the maximum values rounded up to the nearest integer. This causes the bounds to expand as necessary so that its boundaries are integer-aligned.

This is the immutable form of the function roundOut(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: roundedIn() : Bounds2
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with the minimum values rounded up to the nearest integer, and the maximum values rounded down to the nearest integer. This causes the bounds to contract as necessary so that its boundaries are integer-aligned.

This is the immutable form of the function roundIn(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: transformed( matrix : Matrix3 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box (still axis-aligned) that contains the transformed shape of this bounds, applying the matrix as an affine transformation.

NOTE: bounds.transformed( matrix ).transformed( inverse ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the returned bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

This is the immutable form of the function transform(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: dilated( d : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded on all sides by the specified amount.)

This is the immutable form of the function dilate(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: dilatedX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded horizontally (on the left and right) by the specified amount.

This is the immutable form of the function dilateX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: dilatedY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded vertically (on the top and bottom) by the specified amount.

This is the immutable form of the function dilateY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: dilatedXY( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded on all sides, with different amounts of expansion horizontally and vertically. Will be identical to the bounds returned by calling bounds.dilatedX( x ).dilatedY( y ).

This is the immutable form of the function dilateXY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

@param x - Amount to dilate horizontally (for each side) @param y - Amount to dilate vertically (for each side)

docs.title: eroded( amount : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is contracted on all sides by the specified amount.

This is the immutable form of the function erode(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: erodedX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is contracted horizontally (on the left and right) by the specified amount.

This is the immutable form of the function erodeX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: erodedY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is contracted vertically (on the top and bottom) by the specified amount.

This is the immutable form of the function erodeY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: erodedXY( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is contracted on all sides, with different amounts of contraction horizontally and vertically.

This is the immutable form of the function erodeXY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

@param x - Amount to erode horizontally (for each side) @param y - Amount to erode vertically (for each side)

docs.title: withOffsets( left : number, top : number, right : number, bottom : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded by a specific amount on all sides (or if some offsets are negative, will contract those sides).

This is the immutable form of the function offset(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

@param left - Amount to expand to the left (subtracts from minX) @param top - Amount to expand to the top (subtracts from minY) @param right - Amount to expand to the right (adds to maxX) @param bottom - Amount to expand to the bottom (adds to maxY)

docs.title: shiftedX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Our bounds, translated horizontally by x, returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function shiftX(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: shiftedY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Our bounds, translated vertically by y, returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function shiftY(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: shiftedXY( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Our bounds, translated by (x,y), returned as a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function shift(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: shifted( v : Vector2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text: Returns our bounds, translated by a vector, returned as a copy.

docs.title: blend( bounds : Bounds2, ratio : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Returns an interpolated value of this bounds and the argument.

@param bounds @param ratio - 0 will result in a copy of this, 1 will result in bounds, and in-between controls the amount of each.

docs.title: setMinMax( minX : number, minY : number, maxX : number, maxY : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text: Sets each value for this bounds, and returns itself.

docs.title: setMinX( minX : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Sets the value of minX.

This is the mutable form of the function withMinX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: setMinY( minY : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Sets the value of minY.

This is the mutable form of the function withMinY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: setMaxX( maxX : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Sets the value of maxX.

This is the mutable form of the function withMaxX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: setMaxY( maxY : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Sets the value of maxY.

This is the mutable form of the function withMaxY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: set( bounds : Bounds2Like ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Sets the values of this bounds to be equal to the input bounds.

This is the mutable form of the function copy(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: includeBounds( bounds : Bounds2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input bounds.

This is the mutable form of the function union(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: constrainBounds( bounds : Bounds2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it is the largest bounds contained both in its original bounds and in the input bounds.

This is the mutable form of the function intersection(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addCoordinates( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input point (x,y).

This is the mutable form of the function withCoordinates(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addPoint( point : Vector2 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input point.

This is the mutable form of the function withPoint(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it is guaranteed to include the given x value (if it didn't already). If the x value was already contained, nothing will be done.

This is the mutable form of the function withX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it is guaranteed to include the given y value (if it didn't already). If the y value was already contained, nothing will be done.

This is the mutable form of the function withY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: roundOut() : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that its boundaries are integer-aligned, rounding the minimum boundaries down and the maximum boundaries up (expanding as necessary).

This is the mutable form of the function roundedOut(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: roundIn() : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that its boundaries are integer-aligned, rounding the minimum boundaries up and the maximum boundaries down (contracting as necessary).

This is the mutable form of the function roundedIn(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: transform( matrix : Matrix3 ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it would fully contain a transformed version if its previous value, applying the matrix as an affine transformation.

NOTE: bounds.transform( matrix ).transform( inverse ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

This is the mutable form of the function transformed(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: dilate( d : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Expands this bounds on all sides by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function dilated(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: dilateX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Expands this bounds horizontally (left and right) by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function dilatedX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: dilateY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Expands this bounds vertically (top and bottom) by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function dilatedY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: dilateXY( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Expands this bounds independently in the horizontal and vertical directions. Will be equal to calling bounds.dilateX( x ).dilateY( y ).

This is the mutable form of the function dilatedXY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: erode( d : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Contracts this bounds on all sides by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function eroded(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: erodeX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Contracts this bounds horizontally (left and right) by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function erodedX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: erodeY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Contracts this bounds vertically (top and bottom) by the specified amount.

This is the mutable form of the function erodedY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: erodeXY( x : number, y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Contracts this bounds independently in the horizontal and vertical directions. Will be equal to calling bounds.erodeX( x ).erodeY( y ).

This is the mutable form of the function erodedXY(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: offset( left : number, top : number, right : number, bottom : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Expands this bounds independently for each side (or if some offsets are negative, will contract those sides).

This is the mutable form of the function withOffsets(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

@param left - Amount to expand to the left (subtracts from minX) @param top - Amount to expand to the top (subtracts from minY) @param right - Amount to expand to the right (adds to maxX) @param bottom - Amount to expand to the bottom (adds to maxY)

docs.title: shiftX( x : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Translates our bounds horizontally by x.

This is the mutable form of the function shiftedX(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: shiftY( y : number ) : Bounds2
docs.text:
Translates our bounds vertically by y.

docs.text:
Returns a new Bounds2 object that only contains the specified point (x,y). Useful for being dilated to form a bounding box around a point. Note that the bounds will not be "empty" as it contains (x,y), but it will have zero area. The x and y coordinates can be specified by numbers or with at Vector2

@param x @param y

docs.title: point( x : Vector2 | number, y? : number ) : Bounds2
pool : Pool
docs.text: (readonly)

docs.title: NOTHING : Bounds2
docs.text:
(readonly)

A constant Bounds2 with minimums = \(\infty\), maximums = \(-\infty\), so that it represents "no bounds whatsoever".

This allows us to take the union (union/includeBounds) of this and any other Bounds2 to get the other bounds back, e.g. Bounds2.NOTHING.union( bounds ).equals( bounds ). This object naturally serves as the base case as a union of zero bounds objects.

Additionally, intersections with NOTHING will always return a Bounds2 equivalent to NOTHING.

docs.title: EVERYTHING : Bounds2
docs.text:
(readonly)

A constant Bounds2 with minimums = \(-\infty\), maximums = \(\infty\), so that it represents "all bounds".

This allows us to take the intersection (intersection/constrainBounds) of this and any other Bounds2 to get the other bounds back, e.g. Bounds2.EVERYTHING.intersection( bounds ).equals( bounds ). This object naturally serves as the base case as an intersection of zero bounds objects.

Additionally, unions with EVERYTHING will always return a Bounds2 equivalent to EVERYTHING.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A 3D cuboid-shaped bounded area (bounding box).

There are a number of convenience functions to get locations and points on the Bounds. Currently we do not store these with the Bounds3 instance, since we want to lower the memory footprint.

minX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, and maxZ are actually stored. We don't do x,y,z,width,height,depth because this can't properly express semi-infinite bounds (like a half-plane), or easily handle what Bounds3.NOTHING and Bounds3.EVERYTHING do with the constructive solid areas.

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: isEmpty() : boolean
docs.text: Whether we have negative width, height or depth. Bounds3.NOTHING is a prime example of an empty Bounds3. Bounds with width = height = depth = 0 are considered not empty, since they include the single (0,0,0) point.

docs.title: isFinite() : boolean
docs.text: Whether our minimums and maximums are all finite numbers. This will exclude Bounds3.NOTHING and Bounds3.EVERYTHING.

docs.title: hasNonzeroVolume() : boolean
docs.text: Whether this bounds has a non-zero volume (non-zero positive width, height and depth).

docs.title: isValid() : boolean

docs.title: copy( bounds? : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Creates a copy of this bounds, or if a bounds is passed in, set that bounds's values to ours.

This is the immutable form of the function set(), if a bounds is provided. This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds. @param bounds - If not provided, creates a new Bounds3 with filled in values. Otherwise, fills in the values of the provided bounds so that it equals this bounds.

docs.title: union( bounds : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:

This is the immutable form of the function includeBounds(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: intersection( bounds : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:

This is the immutable form of the function constrainBounds(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: roundedOut() : Bounds3
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with the minimum values rounded down to the nearest integer, and the maximum values rounded up to the nearest integer. This causes the bounds to expand as necessary so that its boundaries are integer-aligned.

This is the immutable form of the function roundOut(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: roundedIn() : Bounds3
docs.text:
A copy of this bounds, with the minimum values rounded up to the nearest integer, and the maximum values rounded down to the nearest integer. This causes the bounds to contract as necessary so that its boundaries are integer-aligned.

This is the immutable form of the function roundIn(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: transformed( matrix : Matrix4 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
A bounding box (still axis-aligned) that contains the transformed shape of this bounds, applying the matrix as an affine transformation.

NOTE: bounds.transformed( matrix ).transformed( inverse ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the returned bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

This is the immutable form of the function transform(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.title: dilated( d : number ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded on all sides by the specified amount.)

This is the immutable form of the function dilate(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds.

docs.text:

docs.title: dilatedY( y : number ) : Bounds3

docs.title: dilatedXYZ( x : number, y : number, z : number ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
A bounding box that is expanded on all sides, with different amounts of expansion along each axis. Will be identical to the bounds returned by calling bounds.dilatedX( x ).dilatedY( y ).dilatedZ( z ).

This is the immutable form of the function dilateXYZ(). This will return a new bounds, and will not modify this bounds. @param x - Amount to dilate horizontally (for each side) @param y - Amount to dilate vertically (for each side) @param z - Amount to dilate depth-wise (for each side)

docs.title: set( bounds : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:

This is the mutable form of the function copy(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: includeBounds( bounds : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input bounds.

This is the mutable form of the function union(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: constrainBounds( bounds : Bounds3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it is the largest bounds contained both in its original bounds and in the input bounds.

This is the mutable form of the function intersection(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addCoordinates( x : number, y : number, z : number ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input point (x,y,z).

This is the mutable form of the function withCoordinates(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: addPoint( point : Vector3 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it contains both its original bounds and the input point.

This is the mutable form of the function withPoint(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: roundOut() : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that its boundaries are integer-aligned, rounding the minimum boundaries down and the maximum boundaries up (expanding as necessary).

This is the mutable form of the function roundedOut(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: roundIn() : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that its boundaries are integer-aligned, rounding the minimum boundaries up and the maximum boundaries down (contracting as necessary).

This is the mutable form of the function roundedIn(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: transform( matrix : Matrix4 ) : Bounds3
docs.text:
Modifies this bounds so that it would fully contain a transformed version if its previous value, applying the matrix as an affine transformation.

NOTE: bounds.transform( matrix ).transform( inverse ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

This is the mutable form of the function transformed(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: dilate( d : number ) : Bounds3

docs.title: dilateY( y : number ) : Bounds3

docs.title: dilateZ( z : number ) : Bounds3

docs.text:
Expands this bounds independently along each axis. Will be equal to calling bounds.dilateX( x ).dilateY( y ).dilateZ( z ).

This is the mutable form of the function dilatedXYZ(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

docs.title: erode( d : number ) : Bounds3
docs.text:

This is the mutable form of the function eroded(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

This is the mutable form of the function erodedXYZ(). This will mutate (change) this bounds, in addition to returning this bounds itself.

@param x - The minimum value of X for the bounds. @param y - The minimum value of Y for the bounds. @param z - The minimum value of Z for the bounds. @param width - The width (maxX - minX) of the bounds.` @param height - The height (maxY - minY) of the bounds. @param depth - The depth (maxZ - minZ) of the bounds.

docs.title: NOTHING : Bounds3
docs.text:
(readonly)

A constant Bounds3 with minimums = \(\infty\), maximums = \(-\infty\), so that it represents "no bounds whatsoever".

This allows us to take the union (union/includeBounds) of this and any other Bounds3 to get the other bounds back, e.g. Bounds3.NOTHING.union( bounds ).equals( bounds ). This object naturally serves as the base case as a union of zero bounds objects.

Additionally, intersections with NOTHING will always return a Bounds3 equivalent to NOTHING.

@constant {Bounds3} NOTHING

docs.title: EVERYTHING : Bounds3
docs.text:
(readonly)

A constant Bounds3 with minimums = \(-\infty\), maximums = \(\infty\), so that it represents "all bounds".

This allows us to take the intersection (intersection/constrainBounds) of this and any other Bounds3 to get the other bounds back, e.g. Bounds3.EVERYTHING.intersection( bounds ).equals( bounds ). This object naturally serves as the base case as an intersection of zero bounds objects.

Additionally, unions with EVERYTHING will always return a Bounds3 equivalent to EVERYTHING.

@constant {Bounds3} EVERYTHING

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Describes a 1d complete (fully defined for any number) function, where values are extrapolated given the final end points.

E.g. if the points (0,0) and (1,1) are provided, it represents the function f(x) = x for ALL values, especially values outside of the range [0,1]. For example, f(6) = 6.

If a single point is provided, it represents a constant function.

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: Class CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: import { CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: findMatchingPair( x : number ) : [ Vector2, Vector2 ]
docs.text:
Returns the pair of points that the x value is defined by.

NOTE: x may NOT be contained in these points, if it's either less than or greater than any points in the points list.

docs.title: evaluate( x : number ) : number
docs.text: Evaluates the function at the given x value, e.g. returns f(x).

docs.title: plus( linearFunction : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Returns a CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction that's the result of adding the two functions.

docs.title: minus( linearFunction : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Returns a CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction that's the result of subtracting the two functions.

docs.title: min( linearFunction : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Returns a CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction that's the result of taking the minimum of the two functions

docs.title: max( linearFunction : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Returns a CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction that's the result of taking the maximum of the two functions

docs.title: withXValues( xValues : number[] ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Allows redefining or clamping/truncating the function by only representing it from the given x values

docs.title: inverted() : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Returns an inverted form of the function (assuming it is monotonically increasing or monotonically decreasing)

docs.title: linear( a : number, b : number ) : CompletePiecewiseLinearFunction
docs.text: Represents the function ax+b

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A complex number with mutable and immutable methods.

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu> @author Chris Malley (PixelZoom, Inc.) @author Matt Pennington (PhET Interactive Simulations) @author Sam Reid (PhET Interactive Simulations)

docs.title: Class Complex
docs.text: import { Complex } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: new Complex( real : number, imaginary : number )
copy( complex? : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Creates a copy of this complex, or if a complex is passed in, set that complex's values to ours.

This is the immutable form of the function set(), if a complex is provided. This will return a new complex, and will not modify this complex.

@param [complex] - If not provided, creates a new Complex with filled in values. Otherwise, fills in the values of the provided complex so that it equals this complex.

docs.title: phase() : number
docs.text: The phase / argument of the complex number.

docs.title: getMagnitude() : number
docs.text: The magnitude (Euclidean/L2 Norm) of this complex number, i.e. \(\sqrt{a^2+b^2}\).

docs.title: getMagnitudeSquared() : number
docs.text: The squared magnitude (square of the Euclidean/L2 Norm) of this complex, i.e. \(a^2+b^2\).

docs.title: getArgument() : number
docs.text: Returns the argument of this complex number (immutable)

docs.title: equals( other : Complex ) : boolean
docs.text:
Exact equality comparison between this Complex and another Complex.

@returns Whether the two complex numbers have equal components

docs.title: equalsEpsilon( other : Complex, epsilon ) : boolean
docs.text:
Approximate equality comparison between this Complex and another Complex.

@returns - Whether difference between the two complex numbers has no component with an absolute value greater than epsilon.

docs.title: plus( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Addition of this Complex and another Complex, returning a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function add(). This will return a new Complex, and will not modify this Complex.

docs.title: minus( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Subtraction of this Complex by another Complex c, returning a copy.

This is the immutable form of the function subtract(). This will return a new Complex, and will not modify this Complex.

docs.title: times( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text: Complex multiplication. Immutable version of multiply

docs.title: dividedBy( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text: Complex division. Immutable version of divide

docs.title: negated() : Complex
docs.text: Complex negation Immutable version of negate

docs.title: sqrtOf() : Complex
docs.text: Square root. Immutable form of sqrt.

docs.title: powerByReal( realPower : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Returns the power of this complex number by a real number.

docs.title: sinOf() : Complex
docs.text: Sine. Immutable form of sin.

docs.title: cosOf() : Complex
docs.text: Cosine. Immutable form of cos.

docs.title: squared() : Complex
docs.text: Returns the square of this complex number and does not modify it. This is the immutable version of square.

docs.title: conjugated() : Complex
docs.text: Complex conjugate. Immutable form of conjugate

docs.title: exponentiated() : Complex
docs.text: Takes e to the power of this complex number. \(e^{a+bi}=e^a\cos b + i\sin b\). This is the immutable form of exponentiate.

docs.title: setRealImaginary( real : number, imaginary : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Sets all components of this complex, returning this

docs.title: setReal( real : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Sets the real component of this complex, returning this

docs.title: setImaginary( imaginary : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Sets the imaginary component of this complex, returning this

docs.title: set( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Sets the components of this complex to be a copy of the parameter

This is the mutable form of the function copy(). This will mutate (change) this complex, in addition to returning this complex itself.

docs.title: setPolar( magnitude : number, phase : number ) : Complex
docs.text:
Sets this Complex's value to be the a,b values matching the given magnitude and phase (in radians), changing this Complex, and returning itself.

@param magnitude @param phase - In radians

docs.title: add( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Addition of this Complex and another Complex, returning a copy.

This is the mutable form of the function plus(). This will modify and return this.

docs.title: subtract( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text:
Subtraction of another Complex from this Complex, returning a copy.

This is the mutable form of the function minus(). This will modify and return this.

docs.title: multiply( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text: Mutable Complex multiplication.

docs.title: divide( c : Complex ) : Complex
docs.text: Mutable Complex division. The immutable form is dividedBy.

docs.title: negate() : Complex
docs.text: Mutable Complex negation

docs.title: exponentiate() : Complex
docs.text: Sets this Complex to e to the power of this complex number. \(e^{a+bi}=e^a\cos b + i\sin b\). This is the mutable version of exponentiated

docs.title: square() : Complex
docs.text: Squares this complex number. This is the mutable version of squared.

docs.title: sqrt() : Complex
docs.text: Square root. Mutable form of sqrtOf.

docs.title: sin() : Complex
docs.text: Sine. Mutable form of sinOf.

docs.title: cos() : Complex
docs.text: Cosine. Mutable form of cosOf.

docs.title: conjugate() : Complex
docs.text: Complex conjugate. Mutable form of conjugated

docs.title: getCubeRoots() : Complex[]
docs.text: Returns the cube roots of this complex number.

docs.title: toString() : string
docs.text: Debugging string for the complex number (provides real and imaginary parts).

docs.title: real : number
docs.text: The real part. For a complex number \(a+bi\), this is \(a\).

docs.title: imaginary : number
docs.text: The imaginary part. For a complex number \(a+bi\), this is \(b\).

docs.title: real( real : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Constructs a complex number from just the real part (assuming the imaginary part is 0).

docs.title: imaginary( imaginary : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Constructs a complex number from just the imaginary part (assuming the real part is 0).

docs.title: createPolar( magnitude : number, phase : number ) : Complex
docs.text: Constructs a complex number from the polar form. For a magnitude \(r\) and phase \(\varphi\), this will be \(\cos\varphi+i r\sin\varphi\).

docs.title: solveLinearRoots( a : Complex, b : Complex ) : Complex[] | null
docs.text:
Returns an array of the roots of the quadratic equation \(ax + b=0\), or null if every value is a solution.

@returns The roots of the equation, or null if all values are roots.

docs.title: solveQuadraticRoots( a : Complex, b : Complex, c : Complex ) : Complex[] | null
docs.text:
Returns an array of the roots of the quadratic equation \(ax^2 + bx + c=0\), or null if every value is a solution.

@returns The roots of the equation, or null if all values are roots (if multiplicity>1, returns multiple copies)

docs.title: solveCubicRoots( a : Complex, b : Complex, c : Complex, d : Complex ) : Complex[] | null
docs.text:
Returns an array of the roots of the cubic equation \(ax^3 + bx^2 + cx + d=0\), or null if every value is a solution.

@returns The roots of the equation, or null if all values are roots (if multiplicity>1, returns multiple copies)

docs.title: ZERO : Complex
docs.text:
(readonly)

Immutable constant \(0\). @constant

docs.title: ONE : Complex
docs.text:
(readonly)

Immutable constant \(1\). @constant

docs.title: I : Complex
docs.text:
(readonly)

Immutable constant \(i\), the imaginary unit. @constant

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Construction of 2D convex hulls from a list of points.

For example:

docs.title: on
docs.text: var points = _.range( 50 ).map( function() { return new phet.dot.Vector2( 5 + ( 256 - 10 ) * Math.random(), 5 + ( 128 - 10 ) * Math.random() ); } ); var hullPoints = phet.dot.ConvexHull2.grahamScan( points, false );

docs.title: off
docs.text:
context.beginPath(); hullPoints.forEach( function( point ) { context.lineTo( point.x, point.y ); } ); context.closePath(); context.fillStyle = '#eee'; context.fill(); context.strokeStyle = '#f00'; context.stroke();

context.beginPath(); points.forEach( function( point ) { context.arc( point.x, point.y, 2, 0, Math.PI * 2, false ); context.closePath(); } ); context.fillStyle = '#00f'; context.fill();

docs.title: end canvasExample
docs.text: @author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: Source Code
docs.text: See the source for ConvexHull2.ts in the dot repository.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Handles constrained Delaunay triangulation based on "Sweep-line algorithm for constrained Delaunay triangulation" by Domiter and Zalik (2008), with some details provided by "An efficient sweep-line Delaunay triangulation algorithm" by Zalik (2005).

TODO: Second (basin) heuristic not yet implemented. https://github.com/phetsims/dot/issues/96 TODO: Constraints not yet implemented. TODO: Check number of triangles/edges/vertices with Euler's Formula TODO: Handle "outside" cases (and changing the front edges) for constrained edges

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: Class DelaunayTriangulation
docs.text: import { DelaunayTriangulation } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: step()
docs.text: Moves the triangulation forward by a vertex. @private

docs.title: fillBorderTriangle( firstEdge, secondEdge, firstSideVertex, middleVertex, secondSideVertex )
docs.text:
Builds a triangle between two vertices. @private

@param {Edge} firstEdge @param {Edge} secondEdge @param {Vertex} firstSideVertex @param {Vertex} middleVertex @param {Vertex} secondSideVertex @returns {Edge} - The newly created edge

docs.title: reconnectFrontEdges( oldRightEdge, oldLeftEdge, newRightEdge, newLeftEdge )
docs.text:
Disconnects a section of front edges, and connects a new section. @private

Disconnects: <nextEdge> (cut) <oldLeftEdge> ..... <oldRightEdge> (cut) <previousEdge>

Connects: <nextEdge> (join) <newLeftEdge> ..... <newRightEdge> (join) <previousEdge>

If previousEdge is null, we'll need to set our firstFrontEdge to the newRightEdge.

@param {Edge} oldRightEdge @param {Edge} oldLeftEdge @param {Edge} newRightEdge @param {Edge} newLeftEdge

docs.title: addHalfPiHeuristic( rightFrontEdge, leftFrontEdge )
docs.text:
Tries to fill in acute angles with triangles after we add a vertex into the front. @private

@param {Edge} rightFrontEdge @param {Edge} leftFrontEdge

docs.title: constrainEdges( vertex, rightFrontEdge, leftFrontEdge )
docs.text:
Handles any "edge events" that delete intersecting edges, creating the new edge, and filling in (all only if necessary). @private

@param {Vertex} vertex @param {Edge} rightFrontEdge @param {Edge} leftFrontEdge

docs.title: triangulatePolygon( edges )
docs.text:
Creates edges/triangles to triangulate a simple polygon. @private

@param {Array.<Edge>} edges - Should be in counterclockwise order

docs.title: finalize()
docs.text: Should be called when there are no more remaining vertices left to be processed. @private

docs.title: legalizeEdge( edge )
docs.text:
Checks an edge to see whether its two adjacent triangles satisfy the delaunay condition (the far point of one triangle should not be contained in the other triangle's circumcircle), and if it is not satisfied, flips the edge so the condition is satisfied. @private

@param {Edge} edge

docs.title: vertexComparison( a, b )
docs.text:
Comparison for sorting points by y, then by x. @private

TODO: Do we need to reverse the x sort? "If our edge is horizontal, the ending point with smaller x coordinate https://github.com/phetsims/dot/issues/96 is considered as the upper point"?

@param {Vertex} a @param {Vertex} b @returns {number}

docs.title: vertexProduct( sharedVertex, aVertex, bVertex )
docs.text:
Returns the cross product of (aVertex-sharedVertex) and (bVertex-sharedVertex) @private

@param {Vertex} sharedVertex @param {Vertex} aVertex @param {Vertex} bVertex @returns {number}

docs.text: See the source for DelaunayTriangulation.js in the dot repository.

docs.title: Class EigenvalueDecomposition
docs.text:
Eigensystem decomposition, based on Jama (http://math.nist.gov/javanumerics/jama/)

Eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a real matrix. <P> If A is symmetric, then A = V*D*V' where the eigenvalue matrix D is diagonal and the eigenvector matrix V is orthogonal. I.e. A = V.times(D.times(V.transpose())) and V.times(V.transpose()) equals the identity matrix. <P> If A is not symmetric, then the eigenvalue matrix D is block diagonal with the real eigenvalues in 1-by-1 blocks and any complex eigenvalues, lambda + i*mu, in 2-by-2 blocks, [lambda, mu; -mu, lambda]. The columns of V represent the eigenvectors in the sense that A*V = V*D, i.e. A.times(V) equals V.times(D). The matrix V may be badly conditioned, or even singular, so the validity of the equation A = V*D*inverse(V) depends upon V.cond().

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

import { EigenvalueDecomposition } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: isIdentity() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether this matrix is an identity matrix.

docs.title: isFastIdentity() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether this matrix is likely to be an identity matrix (returning false means "inconclusive, may be identity or not"), but true is guaranteed to be an identity matrix.

docs.title: isTranslation() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether this matrix is a translation matrix. By this we mean it has no shear, rotation, or scaling It may be a translation of zero.

docs.title: isAffine() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether this matrix is an affine matrix (e.g. no shear).

docs.title: isAligned() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether it's an affine matrix where the components of transforms are independent, i.e. constructed from arbitrary component scaling and translation.

docs.title: isAxisAligned() : boolean
docs.text:
Returns if it's an affine matrix where the components of transforms are independent, but may be switched (unlike isAligned)

i.e. the 2x2 rotational sub-matrix is of one of the two forms: A 0 or 0 A 0 B B 0 This means that moving a transformed point by (x,0) or (0,y) will result in a motion along one of the axes.

docs.title: isFinite() : boolean
docs.text: Returns whether every single entry in this matrix is a finite number (non-NaN, non-infinite).

docs.title: getDeterminant() : number
docs.text: Returns the determinant of this matrix.

docs.title: getTranslation() : Vector2
docs.text: Returns the 2D translation, assuming multiplication with a homogeneous vector

docs.title: getScaleVector() : Vector2
docs.text: Returns a vector that is equivalent to ( T(1,0).magnitude(), T(0,1).magnitude() ) where T is a relative transform

docs.title: getSignedScale() : number
docs.text: Returns the total "amount" of scaled area in this matrix (which will be negative if it flips the coordinate system). For instance, Matrix3.scaling( 2 ) will return 4, since it scales the area by 4.

docs.title: getRotation() : number
docs.text: Returns the angle in radians for the 2d rotation from this matrix, between pi, -pi

docs.title: toMatrix4() : Matrix4
docs.text: Returns an identity-padded copy of this matrix with an increased dimension.

docs.title: toAffineMatrix4() : Matrix4
docs.text: Returns an identity-padded copy of this matrix with an increased dimension, treating this matrix's affine components only.

docs.title: toString() : string
docs.text: Returns a string form of this object

docs.title: toSVGMatrix() : SVGMatrix
docs.text: Creates an SVG form of this matrix, for high-performance processing in SVG output.

docs.title: getCSSTransform() : string
docs.text: Returns the CSS form (simplified if possible) for this transformation matrix.

docs.title: getSVGTransform() : string
docs.text: Returns the CSS-like SVG matrix form for this transformation matrix.

docs.title: multiplyTransposeVector2( v : Vector2 ) : Vector2

docs.title: multiplyRelativeVector2( v : Vector2 ) : Vector2
docs.text:
Sets the vector to the result of (matrix * vector - matrix * zero). Since this is a homogeneous operation, it is equivalent to the multiplication of (x,y,0).

@returns - The vector that was mutated

docs.text:
Multiplication of this quaternion by another quaternion, returning a copy. Multiplication is also known at the Hamilton Product (an extension of the cross product for vectors) The product of two rotation quaternions will be equivalent to a rotation by the first quaternion followed by the second quaternion rotation, @public

@param {Vector3} v @returns {Vector3}

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Random number generator with an optional seed. It uses seedrandom.js, a monkey patch for Math, see https://github.com/davidbau/seedrandom.

If you are developing a PhET Simulation, you should probably use the global DOT/dotRandom because it provides built-in support for phet-io seeding and a check that it isn't used before the seed has been set.

@author John Blanco (PhET Interactive Simulations) @author Aaron Davis (PhET Interactive Simulations) @author Sam Reid (PhET Interactive Simulations) @author Mohamed Safi

docs.title: Class Random
docs.text: import { Random } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: dispose()
docs.text: Clears out this instance from all the Random instances.

docs.title: getSeed() : number | null
docs.text: Gets the seed.

docs.title: nextBoolean() : boolean
docs.text: Returns the next pseudo-random boolean

docs.title: nextInt( n : number ) : number
docs.text: Returns the next pseudo random number from this random number generator sequence. The random number is an integer ranging from 0 to n-1. @returns an integer

docs.title: nextIntBetween( min : number, max : number ) : number
docs.text: Randomly select a random integer between min and max (inclusive). @param min - must be an integer @param max - must be an integer @returns an integer between min and max, inclusive

docs.title: sample( array : T[] ) : T
docs.text: Randomly select one element from the given array. @param array - from which one element will be selected, must have at least one element @returns the selected element from the array

docs.title: shuffle( array : T[] ) : T[]
docs.text: Creates an array of shuffled values, using a version of the Fisher-Yates shuffle. Adapted from lodash-2.4.1 by Sam Reid on Aug 16, 2016, See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fisher-Yates_shuffle. @param array - the array which will be shuffled @returns a new array with all the same elements in the passed-in array, in randomized order.

docs.title: nextDouble() : number
docs.text: Returns the next pseudo random number from this random number generator sequence in the range [0, 1) The distribution of the random numbers is uniformly distributed across the interval @returns the random number

docs.title: nextDoubleBetween( min : number, max : number ) : number
docs.text: Randomly selects a double in the range [min,max).

docs.title: nextGaussian() : number
docs.text: Returns the next gaussian-distributed random number from this random number generator sequence. The distribution of the random numbers is gaussian, with a mean = 0 and standard deviation = 1

docs.title: nextDoubleInRange( range : Range ) : number
docs.text: Gets the next random double in a Range. For min < max, the return value is [min,max), between min (inclusive) and max (exclusive). For min === max, the return value is min.

docs.title: nextPointInBounds( bounds : Bounds2 ) : Vector2
docs.text: Gets a random point within the provided Bounds2, [min,max)

docs.title: setSeed( seed : number | null )
docs.text: @param seed - if null, Math.random will be used to create the seed.

docs.title: sampleProbabilities( weights : readonly number[] ) : number
docs.text: Choose a numeric index from the array of weights. The array of weights does not need to be normalized. See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/8877249/generate-random-integers-with-probabilities See also ContinuousServer.weightedSampleTest which uses the same algorithm

docs.title: numberOfCalls
docs.text: the number of times nextDouble is called. Clients should not write to this value.

docs.title: Source Code
docs.text: See the source for Random.ts in the dot repository.

docs.title: union( range : Range ) : Range
docs.text:
REVIEW: The naming is not helping me understand that this function is just the immutable version of includeRange().

The smallest range that contains both this range and the input range, returned as a copy.

The method below is the immutable form of the function includeRange(). The method will return a new range, and will not modify this range.

docs.title: intersection( range : Range ) : Range
docs.text:
REVIEW: The naming is not helping me understand that this function is just the immutable version of constrainRange().

The smallest range that is contained by both this range and the input range, returned as a copy.

The method below the immutable form of the function constrainRange(). The method below will return a new range, and will not modify this range.

docs.title: includeRange( range : Range ) : Range
docs.text:
Modifies this range so that it contains both its original range and the input range.

This is the mutable form of the function union(). This will mutate (change) this range, in addition to returning this range itself.

docs.title: constrainRange( range : Range ) : Range
docs.text:
Modifies this range so that it is the largest range contained both in its original range and in the input range.

This is the mutable form of the function intersection(). This will mutate (change) this range, in addition to returning this range itself.

docs.title: shifted( n : number ) : Range
docs.text:
REVIEW: do we also need a mutable form of shifted?

Returns a new range that is the same as this range, but shifted by the specified amount.

docs.text: Converts the attributes of this range to a string

docs.title: constrainValue( value : number ) : number
docs.text: Constrains a value to the range.

docs.title: times( value : number ) : Range
docs.text: Multiply the min and max by the provided value, immutable

docs.title: multiply( value : number ) : this
docs.text: Multiply the min and max by the provided value, mutable

docs.title: equals( object : IntentionalAny ) : boolean
docs.text: Determines if this Range is equal to some object.

docs.title: equalsEpsilon( object : IntentionalAny, epsilon : number ) : boolean
docs.text: Determines if this Range is approximately equal to some object.

docs.title: getNormalizedValue( value : number ) : number
docs.text: Given a value, normalize it to this Range's length, returning a value between 0 and 1 for values contained in the Range. If the value is not contained in Range, then the return value will not be between 0 and 1.

docs.title: expandNormalizedValue( normalizedValue : number ) : number
docs.text: Compute the opposite of a normalized value. Given a normalized value (between 0 and 1). Worked with any number though, (even outside of the range). It is the client's responsibility to clamp if that is important to the usage.

docs.title: clampDelta( value : number, delta : number ) : number
docs.text: Given a value and a delta to change that value, clamp the delta to make sure the value stays within range.

docs.text:
A sphere in 3 dimensions (NOT a 3-sphere).

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: Class Sphere3
docs.text: import { Sphere3 } from 'scenerystack/dot';

docs.title: intersect( ray, epsilon )
docs.text:
Determines if a ray (a half-line) intersects this sphere. A successful intersection returns the result the closest intersection in the form { distance, hitPoint, normal, fromOutside }, distance: {number} distance to the intersection point hitPoint: {Vector3} the intersection point normal: {Vector3} the normal vector on the sphere at the point of intersection. (the normal vector points outwards the sphere by convention) fromOutside: {boolean} is the ray half-line intersecting the sphere from the outside of a sphere or from the inside.

Returns null if the ray misses the sphere

@public @param {Ray3} ray - The ray to intersect with the sphere @param {number} epsilon - A small varying-point value to be used to handle intersections tangent to the sphere @returns {{ distance: number, hitPoint: Vector3, normal, fromOutside: boolean }| null}

docs.title: intersections( ray, epsilon )
docs.text:
Returns the intersections of a ray with a sphere. There will be 0 or 2 intersections, with the "proper" intersection first, if applicable (closest in front of the ray). Note that this method makes the implicit assumptions that the ray's origin does not lie inside the sphere.

@public @param {Ray3} ray - The ray to intersect with the sphere @param {number} epsilon - A small varying-point value to be used to handle intersections tangent to the sphere @returns {Array.<{distance:number, hitPoint:Vector3, normal:Vector3, fromOutside:boolean }>| null} - An array of intersection results like { distance, hitPoint, normal, fromOutside }.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Forward and inverse transforms with 3x3 matrices. Methods starting with 'transform' will apply the transform from our primary matrix, while methods starting with 'inverse' will apply the transform from the inverse of our matrix.

Generally, this means transform.inverseThing( transform.transformThing( thing ) ).equals( thing ).

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>

docs.title: transformPosition2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector like it is a point with a position (translation is applied). @public

For an affine matrix \(M\), the result is the homogeneous multiplication \(M\begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\).

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: transformDelta2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector like position is irrelevant (translation is not applied). @public

For an affine matrix \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & c \\ d & e & f \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\), the result is \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & 0 \\ d & e & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\).

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: transformNormal2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector like it is a normal to a curve (so that the curve is transformed, and the new normal to the curve at the transformed point is returned). @public

For an affine matrix \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & c \\ d & e & f \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\), the result is \(\begin{bmatrix} a & e & 0 \\ d & b & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}^{-1} \begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\). This is essentially the transposed inverse with translation removed.

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: transformX( x )
docs.text:
Returns the resulting x-coordinate of the transformation of all vectors with the initial input x-coordinate. If this is not well-defined (the x value depends on y), an assertion is thrown (and y is assumed to be 0). @public

@param {number} x @returns {number}

docs.title: transformY( y )
docs.text:
Returns the resulting y-coordinate of the transformation of all vectors with the initial input y-coordinate. If this is not well-defined (the y value depends on x), an assertion is thrown (and x is assumed to be 0). @public

@param {number} y @returns {number}

docs.title: transformDeltaX( x )
docs.text:
Returns the x-coordinate difference for two transformed vectors, which add the x-coordinate difference of the input x (and same y values) beforehand. @public

@param {number} x @returns {number}

docs.title: transformDeltaY( y )
docs.text:
Returns the y-coordinate difference for two transformed vectors, which add the y-coordinate difference of the input y (and same x values) beforehand. @public

@param {number} y @returns {number}

docs.title: transformBounds2( bounds )
docs.text:
Returns bounds (axis-aligned) that contains the transformed bounds rectangle. @public

NOTE: transform.inverseBounds2( transform.transformBounds2( bounds ) ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the returned bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

@param {Bounds2} bounds @returns {Bounds2}

docs.title: transformShape( shape )
docs.text:
Returns a transformed phet.kite.Shape. @public

@param {Shape} shape @returns {Shape}

docs.title: transformRay2( ray )
docs.text:
Returns a transformed ray. @public

@param {Ray2} ray @returns {Ray2}

docs.title: inversePosition2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector by the inverse of our transform like it is a point with a position (translation is applied). @public

For an affine matrix \(M\), the result is the homogeneous multiplication \(M^{-1}\begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\).

This is the inverse of transformPosition2().

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: inverseDelta2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector by the inverse of our transform like position is irrelevant (translation is not applied). @public

For an affine matrix \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & c \\ d & e & f \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\), the result is \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & 0 \\ d & e & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}^{-1} \begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\).

This is the inverse of transformDelta2().

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: inverseNormal2( v )
docs.text:
Transforms a 2-dimensional vector by the inverse of our transform like it is a normal to a curve (so that the curve is transformed, and the new normal to the curve at the transformed point is returned). @public

For an affine matrix \(\begin{bmatrix} a & b & c \\ d & e & f \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\), the result is \(\begin{bmatrix} a & e & 0 \\ d & b & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}\). This is essentially the transposed transform with translation removed.

This is the inverse of transformNormal2().

@param {Vector2} v @returns {Vector2}

docs.title: inverseX( x )
docs.text:
Returns the resulting x-coordinate of the inverse transformation of all vectors with the initial input x-coordinate. If this is not well-defined (the x value depends on y), an assertion is thrown (and y is assumed to be 0). @public

This is the inverse of transformX().

@param {number} x @returns {number}

docs.title: inverseY( y )
docs.text:
Returns the resulting y-coordinate of the inverse transformation of all vectors with the initial input y-coordinate. If this is not well-defined (the y value depends on x), an assertion is thrown (and x is assumed to be 0). @public

This is the inverse of transformY().

@param {number} y @returns {number}

docs.title: inverseDeltaX( x )
docs.text:
Returns the x-coordinate difference for two inverse-transformed vectors, which add the x-coordinate difference of the input x (and same y values) beforehand. @public

This is the inverse of transformDeltaX().

@param {number} x @returns {number}

docs.title: inverseDeltaY( y )
docs.text:
Returns the y-coordinate difference for two inverse-transformed vectors, which add the y-coordinate difference of the input y (and same x values) beforehand. @public

This is the inverse of transformDeltaY().

@param {number} y @returns {number}

docs.title: inverseBounds2( bounds )
docs.text:
Returns bounds (axis-aligned) that contains the inverse-transformed bounds rectangle. @public

NOTE: transform.inverseBounds2( transform.transformBounds2( bounds ) ) may be larger than the original box, if it includes a rotation that isn't a multiple of \(\pi/2\). This is because the returned bounds may expand in area to cover ALL of the corners of the transformed bounding box.

@param {Bounds2} bounds @returns {Bounds2}

docs.title: inverseShape( shape )
docs.text:
Returns an inverse-transformed phet.kite.Shape. @public

This is the inverse of transformShape()

@param {Shape} shape @returns {Shape}

docs.title: inverseRay2( ray )
docs.text:
Returns an inverse-transformed ray. @public

This is the inverse of transformRay2()

@param {Ray2} ray @returns {Ray2}

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Forward and inverse transforms with 4x4 matrices, allowing flexibility including affine and perspective transformations.

Methods starting with 'transform' will apply the transform from our primary matrix, while methods starting with 'inverse' will apply the transform from the inverse of our matrix.

Generally, this means transform.inverseThing( transform.transformThing( thing ) ).equals( thing ).

@author Jonathan Olson <jonathan.olson@colorado.edu>
